w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Seismic, Injection (Saltwater Deposit-SWD), and Frac Data Buckets
This document restructures the data plan around the three buckets requested for the PoC: Seismic, Injection (Saltwater Deposit-SWD), and Frac.
Each data source section is structured as: Data category → Source name → Official access links → Delaware trim guidance → Field table.
starred rows (green shading) = Important PoC fields. Non-starred rows are preserved in raw storage but are not required for the first-pass attribution workflow.

Yes. ArcGIS REST service supports bounding-box and area-of-interest spatial queries. Apply geometry filter for Delaware Basin counties (Culberson, Reeves, Loving, Ward, Winkler, Pecos) in the curated layer.
Authoritative Texas-specific catalog with direct Delaware coverage, event export to CSV, and programmatic REST access for event selection and evidence display. Primary seismic source for the PoC.
REST query for app ingestion. CSV export for manual validation and backfill. Use bounding box: approx.
ArcGIS internal object ID — pagination and debugging only.
Primary event identifier for event selection, event detail retrieval, and evidence object lineage. The PoC event-list and analysis endpoints are keyed on this field.
Used in the event list ranking, event card display, and attribution summary context. Required for filtering events above a meaningful threshold.
Preserves reported magnitude type (ML, Mw, etc.) for scientific integrity in the evidence view.
Required for event map display and spatial joins to nearby SWD / frac activity within configurable search radii.
Required for event map display and spatial joins to nearby SWD / frac activity.
Injection depth vs. event depth comparison is central to the Delaware Basin attribution workflow. Shallow injection (0.5–3 km) vs. deep basement events (~5–8 km) distinction.
Confidence-support field explaining event solution strength in the evidence view.
Method metadata for technical review only.
Filter to retain 'earthquake' events only; exclude quarry blasts and other non-target classes.
Supports Delaware trim verification and user-facing event location labels.
Optional quality signal for the evidence object.
Optional quality signal for the evidence object.
Core event timestamp. Drives the attribution analysis window and all temporal joins to SWD and frac records.
Automatic vs. manual — technical review only.
Distinguishes reviewed vs. preliminary events. Prefer reviewed records for PoC demo events.
Primary Delaware trim field. Cupertino, Reeves, Loving, Ward, Winkler, Pecos counties define the Delaware slice.
Ingestion delta handling and audit trail.
Optional focal mechanism context. Not required for first-pass PoC.
Waveform fit quality metric. Supports confidence framing in the result card.
Station count is a key support signal for event confidence and the evidence view.
Native geometry for map rendering and spatial joins to SWD/frac layers.
Yes. Query by bounding box, radius, time range, and magnitude threshold. Retain only Delaware county slice after ingest.
for event identifiers, timestamps, magnitude, and uncertainty metadata. Useful for event reconciliation and coverage before
query endpoint. Detail links used only when deeper moment-tensor or product metadata is needed.
Primary USGS event ID. Preserved for source lineage and cross-catalog reconciliation with
Magnitude for display, filtering, and event context.
Human-readable event location label for the UI event card.
Core timestamp for event ordering and temporal joins. Convert to UTC ISO before storage.
Reference link back to the USGS event page.
Detail-feed URL for optional enrichment if deeper metadata is needed.
Public impact field — not required for PoC attribution.
Intensity fields — ancillary, not required for PoC.
Reviewed / automatic / deleted — used for quality handling and preferring reviewed events.
Not relevant to the Delaware Basin PoC.
Comma-separated alternate IDs. Used for reconciling events across catalogs.
Closest station distance — optional quality metric.
Waveform fit metric for confidence framing, parallel to
Azimuthal gap — location uncertainty proxy for support framing in the evidence view.
Retain 'earthquake' events; filter all other types.
Longitude for map display and spatial joins.
Latitude for map display and spatial joins.
Depth in km — critical for injection-depth vs. event-depth comparison in the attribution engine.
), station, channel, and time window anchored on the selected event's latitude/longitude and
. Filter to stations within a configurable radius of the Delaware Basin.

Provides station-level waveform metadata and instrument response data needed to resolve physical ground-motion amplitude. Source-receiver distance (event latitude/longitude vs. station latitude/longitude) is used by the attribution engine to assess which stations constrain the event location and which nearby operations most directly influenced the recorded wavefield. Also enables cross-verification of
waveform segments. Both are REST services with open access for
). Required for waveform data retrieval and station identification.
Station code within the network. Needed to resolve contributing stations per event.
Channel code (e.g., HHZ, HHN, HHE). Identifies component and sampling rate.
Window start for waveform retrieval, anchored around
Station latitude. Used to compute source-receiver distance for the attribution engine.
Station longitude. Used to compute source-receiver distance.
Instrument sensitivity. Required to convert raw counts to ground motion units (nm/s).
Source 1 — RRC Underground Injection Control (UIC) Inventory & Permit

Yes. Statewide source. Filter to Delaware by county code (Culberson=023, Reeves=137, Loving=169, Ward=185, Winkler=207, Pecos=121) in the curated layer. RRC GIS Viewer can be used to spatially validate the slice.
Authoritative Texas injection/disposal well inventory and permit context. Provides the well registry that the H-10 monthly monitor records are keyed against. Per
et al. 2024, the Delaware Basin has experienced pore pressure increases up to 5 MPa tied to SWD — the UIC inventory establishes which wells are authorized and at what depth.
Bulk ingest from UIC database files. UIC query for spot validation.
Primary UIC control identifier for source lineage and join to monthly monitoring records.
Oil/gas permit context. Used for well interpretation and QA in the evidence view.
Well-number context used in technical review and record matching across RRC datasets.
Operator identifier for evidence display and stakeholder review.
County code — primary Delaware trim key. Map to Culberson, Reeves, Loving, Ward, Winkler, Pecos.
API number — primary cross-source join key into
Disposal into nonproductive zone authority. Relevant when characterizing deep disposal in Delaware Basin.
Injection type — distinguishes disposal from EOR and other injection contexts in the evidence view.
Saltwater injection authorization. Directly relevant to SWD driver interpretation.
Injection zone depth context. Key for distinguishing shallow (0.5–3 km) vs. deep (4–6 km) disposal — central to Delaware Basin attribution workflow per
Location descriptor used to resolve coordinates where direct
Resolved from UIC-LOCATION via RRC GIS Viewer or API data back-fill. Required for map display and spatial join to seismic events.
Resolved from UIC-LOCATION. Required for map display and spatial join.
Active/inactive permit status — helps exclude decommissioned wells from active context.
Source 2 — RRC H-10 Monthly Injection Monitoring
UIC Magnetic Tape User's Guide — H-10 segment
RRC data sets available for download (H-10 within UIC bulk)
Yes. Statewide source. Narrow to Delaware using the linked UIC root record (UIC-CNTY-NO) and spatial filter.

Monthly injected volume and pressure are the central SWD variables for historical attribution. The Delaware Basin had >4 billion barrels injected into shallow formations with documented pressure-front migration (
et al. 2024). Cumulative volume and lag-time derived fields are critical for the attribution engine.
Monthly monitor segment in the UIC bulk file. H-10 public query for validation.
Part of the composite monthly key (century prefix).
Part of the monthly key for temporal alignment to the selected seismic event.
Part of the monthly key for temporal alignment.
Core SWD pressure field for attribution analysis. Pore pressure changes up to 5 MPa have been linked to Delaware Basin seismicity (
Maximum injection pressure supports high-pressure context in the evidence object.
Core monthly injected liquid volume (barrels). Central to the SWD driver attribution workflow.
Only needed where gas-volume monitoring applies.
Running sum of MN-H10-TOTAL-VOL-BBL across months prior to event date. Required to compute cumulative injection load context used in the attribution engine.
Calendar days between well first-injection date and event date. Supports lag-time analysis central to attribution.
Source 3 — RRC Production Data Query (PDQ) — Lease-Level Production
RRC data sets for download — Production DB
Yes. Query by county and district. Historical Ledger covers Jan 1993 to present with 2-month reporting lag. Filter by county codes for Delaware Basin counties.

Oil and gas production volumes are the primary generator of produced water — the feedstock for SWD. High production volumes statistically predict higher SWD injection volumes. Including lease-level production in the evidence object strengthens the causal chain: production → produced water → SWD injection → pressure front → seismicity. Research on the Delaware Basin (Skoumal et al. 2020, PNAS 2021) uses production data alongside injection to contextualize induced seismicity drivers.
PDQ web query for validation. Bulk download from the Production database file for ingestion. Note 2-month reporting lag — account for this in temporal alignment logic.
Lease identifier. Join key to link production volume to a specific well cluster in the Delaware slice.
API well number. Cross-join key to UIC control records and
Operator identifier. Used in evidence display and operator-level aggregation for nearby context.
County code. Delaware trim field (same county map as UIC).
Monthly oil production volume (BBL). High production volumes generate proportional produced-water volumes — produced water is the dominant SWD feedstock in the Delaware Basin.
Monthly gas production volume (MCF). Contextual — supports production-to-disposal ratio calculations.
Condensate volume. Less relevant for induced seismicity workflow.
Monthly produced water volume where reported. Direct proxy for SWD feedstock volume; not always present in PDQ but worth capturing when available.
Display label for operator context only.
records include state (42 = Texas) and county fields. Trim to Delaware counties after ingest.

Practical public source for frac timing, water volume, formation depth, and operator context needed to represent frac activity near a selected seismic event. Research (Aziz Zanjani et al. 2024, The Seismic Record) directly used
to correlate HF activity with seismicity in the southern Delaware Basin.
Bulk CSV/SQL download with data dictionary. Header, additive, ingredient, and water-source tables preserved in raw form. Trim and curate in post-ingest processing.
Primary well-level join key into RRC wellbore, UIC, and production records.
Core field for temporal alignment to the selected seismic event. Used to construct frac timing windows.
Needed for frac window construction and lag calculations relative to event date.
Texas state filter before Delaware county trim.
County code for Delaware trim. Map to Culberson, Reeves, Loving, Ward, Winkler, Pecos.
Operator name for evidence display and stakeholder review.
Required for map display and spatial join to the selected seismic event.
Required for map display and spatial join.
Needed only to validate coordinate normalization to WGS84.
Total vertical depth — optional technical context.
Core frac-intensity field for the evidence object.
Core frac-intensity field — large water volumes can trigger
Well name for user-facing evidence context and operator communication.
Target formation depth. Supports injection-depth vs. event-depth comparison in the attribution engine — critical in Delaware Basin where HF occurs at intermediate depths (2–4 km).
Optional operational context — not required for first-pass attribution.
Not required for attribution. Preserve in raw storage.
Preserve if present; do not block PoC ingestion if missing.
Source 2 — RRC Wellbore Database & Completion Reports (W-2 / G-1)
RRC data sets — Full Wellbore file
Yes. Wellbore query supports county filtering. Full Wellbore bulk file is statewide — trim to Delaware counties in the curated layer.
Provides the completion date, total depth, formation target, and surface coordinates for every wellbore in Texas. These fields cross-validate

does not have a record (pre-2011 or voluntarily unreported jobs), and provide the formation-depth context required to distinguish shallow (Bone Spring, Delaware Mountain Group) from intermediate-depth (
) frac targets — a key distinction in the Delaware Basin attribution workflow.

Full Wellbore bulk download (ASCII/DBF) for ingestion. Wellbore Query for spot validation by API number. Completion form images (PDF) accessible via Oil and Gas Imaged Records Query for detailed review.
Primary API key into all RRC and
Identifies oil, gas, or injection well type. Needed to distinguish frac targets from SWD wells in the same area.
Date of well completion — proxy for the start of frac activity. Used when
job date is missing or needs cross-validation.
Plug date. Used to exclude decommissioned wellbores from the active nearby-context query.
Field identifier. Useful for grouping nearby wells in the evidence view.
Total depth in feet. Used as formation-depth proxy for attribution when
Target formation name (e.g., Delaware Mountain Group,
, Bone Spring). Scientific context for depth interpretation.
Surface latitude. Required for spatial join to seismic event when
Apply Delaware trimming after raw ingest for every statewide or national source. Keep raw source copies unchanged for auditability.
Delaware Basin county codes for RRC: Culberson (023), Reeves (137), Loving (169), Ward (185), Winkler (207), Pecos (121). For USGS and
spatial queries use bounding box approx.
Preserve original source identifiers on every curated record so event-to-evidence traceability is maintained during the PoC.
Selected event list, event detail panel, map-backed evidence
Nearby injection well context for each selected event
Historical volume and pressure evidence for SWD driver assessment
SWD feedstock context — production volume → produced water proxy
Nearby frac timing, water volume, and formation depth for frac-driver assessment
Join of seismic + nearby SWD + nearby frac
Single evidence object returned by the PoC analysis workflow
Source-receiver geometry for attribution engine and station-count verification
This document is intentionally scoped to the Delaware Basin PoC. Three additions beyond the prior plan are recommended:
station metadata and waveform access enables source-receiver geometry computation and cross-validates
network (TX) is openly accessible via the IRIS FDSN services, making this a zero-cost addition.

oil and gas production volumes are the upstream driver of SWD feedstock. Including monthly production in the evidence object allows the attribution result to show the full causal chain from production activity to disposal volume to pore pressure to seismicity — a materially stronger demo for stakeholders.
covers only voluntarily reported jobs from ~2011 onward. The RRC wellbore file fills coverage gaps, cross-validates
job dates via completion dates, and provides formation-depth context that
sometimes omits. Delaware Basin research consistently uses both sources together.
